assertions.assertion: Sets RequireRequestObject = true on the client
Configures a ClientSecret with Type set to IdentityServerConstants.SecretTypes.JsonWebKey or IdentityServerConstants.SecretTypes.X509CertificateBase64
assertions.rationale: Uses new Secret("secret".Sha256()) for the actual client and a vague new Secret("the-public-key-or-cert") without specifying the Type property.

assertions.assertion: Explains that JAR wraps authorization request parameters in a signed JWT making them tamperproof
assertions.rationale: Line 44 states 'JAR makes authorization parameters tamperproof since they're signed.'

assertions.assertion: Mentions that the same key can be shared between client authentication (private_key_jwt) and JAR
assertions.rationale: No mention of key sharing between private_key_jwt and JAR.
